tasks.description: Implement skeleton-config bootstrap per DEC-0001 / FGAP-001. In packages/pi-context/src/context.ts add writeSkeletonConfig (never-clobber, mints substrate_id, writes via writeConfigForDir) + isSkeletonConfig predicate, and change adoptConception's guard to if(existing && !isSkeletonConfig(existing)). In src/index.ts have initProject (248-298) and switchAndCreate (548-575) call writeSkeletonConfig after the dir-scaffold loop and update their completion/notify messages. In src/context-sdk.ts add the 'skeleton' state to the BootstrapStatus union and to deriveBootstrapState's ordered checks. In src/ops-registry.ts update the context-bootstrap-state (and context-init / context-accept-all as needed) description strings. Rework accept-all.test.ts idempotent + 'no defaults' tests, keep the no-config test in context-sdk.test.ts and add a skeleton-state test, add unit tests for isSkeletonConfig + init/switch-c skeleton writes + first amend-config add on a skeleton. Full completion sequence: build, check, test, runtime demo against a throwaway substrate, adversarial probe, regen skills. Implemented by a foreground coding subagent from the approved plan; orchestrator runs all npm and is sole git writer; per-step commit.
tasks.notes: Implemented at ab05a0e (pi-context skeleton-config bootstrap). Build/check/test green; runtime-demoed via the cli (custom path without accept-all; init->accept-all id-preservation + idempotence); a fresh adversarial re-audit found the isSkeletonConfig registry-class fix complete with no new regression.

tasks.description: Implement switch identity reconciliation per DEC-0003 / FGAP-003: switchToExisting and switchAndCreate register config.substrate_id -> active dir via registerSubstrate when the id is absent from the project-root registry; a present-but-different-dir entry continues to raise substrate_id_registry_mismatch. Tests: switch to an unregistered-but-present-config substrate -> registered + context-validate clean; mismatch case preserved. Full completion sequence (build/check/test/runtime-demo/adversarial/skills). Via coding subagent; orchestrator runs all npm + is sole git writer; per-step commit.
tasks.notes: Implemented at 17d9a3b. reconcileActiveSubstrateRegistration wired into all three switch engine functions (switchToExisting, switchAndCreate, switchToPrevious). Build/check/test green; cli runtime demo across all three paths (register-if-absent, mismatch-preserved, switch- heals pruned-previous); a fresh adversarial re-audit (after the probe found switchToPrevious unwired) confirmed all three paths consistent + no-previous error path intact + no new defect. One of the two items in the 0.29.0 cut.

tasks.description: Implement the substrate clone/import arc per DEC-0002 / FGAP-002 / FEAT-001: cloneSubstrate library fn (copy config + all blocks + relations.json + objects/ into the destination dir; mint a fresh substrate_id; record a substrate_derived_from_substrate edge; write a provenance-bearing registry entry) + Pi tool + CLI + orchestrator script as one dual-surface unit; register the substrate_derived_from_substrate relation_type in config; extend the registry schema/type with origin/provenance fields; refine the SoT-drift invariant (context-sdk.ts:1685+) to distinguish native-lost-registry / foreign-import / genuine drift. Full completion sequence. Via coding subagent; orchestrator runs all npm + is sole git writer; per-step commit.

Implement the git merge driver + post-merge integrity pass per DEC-0004 / FGAP-004 / FEAT-002: .gitattributes registration (substrate files -> merge=pi-context) + a [merge "pi-context"] driver entry; the driver entrypoint consuming git's %O/%A/%B/%P and writing a data-model-valid 3-way merge (items matched by id/oid, field-kind dispatch incl. the convergent sequence field via TASK-005, edge-set merge on relations.json, config/schema merge); a post-merge hook / merge-finalize command doing cross-block referential integrity + full invariant + lens revalidation and emitting a typed conflict set. Driver + per-file merge logic live in block-api; the post-merge pass is a git hook / CLI command. Merge-base, branches, refs, cherry-pick are git's, not reimplemented. Full completion sequence. Via coding subagent; orchestrator runs all npm + is sole git writer; per-step commit.

Implement the convergent ordered-sequence field-kind per DEC-0005 / FGAP-005 / FEAT-003: non-interleaving Fugue-class sequence CRDT over item-refs (dense immutable per-element position identifiers, insert mints one id + mutates no neighbor); Kleppmann move-CRDT position register resolved by hybrid logical clock (stable element identity; concurrent same-element moves converge); HLC for replica-independent same-gap ordering + move resolution; sort-on-read projection to a plain ordered ref-array + translation of array-position hand-edits to CRDT ops, through block-api; declared sequence-field kind recognized by schema; CRDT state stored in the owning <block>.json. Plugs into the substrate merge driver's field-merge dispatch. Full completion sequence. Via coding subagent; orchestrator runs all npm + is sole git writer; per-step commit. Depends on the substrate merge driver (TASK-004).

Implement DispatchContext threading through the op-execution contract per DEC-0006 / FGAP-008: add a ctx/writer channel to OpDefinition.run + registerAll (thread the pi ExtensionContext verified identity) + the pi-context-cli dispatch (build DispatchContext from --writer); forward ctx in all 10 ctx-dropping op handlers (append/update/remove-block-item, the nested trio, write-block, append-relation, amend-config, write-schema) and the pi-workflows step-block.ts writers; fix context-archive's declared-but-ignored writer. Full completion sequence. Via coding subagent; orchestrator runs all npm + is sole git writer; per-step commit.
tasks.notes: Implemented at f5b6bd5. Optional ctx?:DispatchContext on OpDefinition.run; cli builds it from --writer/resolveIdentity, in-pi registerAll from auth-gate params.writer else ctx.model agent; 10 write handlers forward it; pi-workflows step-block + artifact write stamp workflow-kind; promote-item/write-schema-migration converged; context-archive writer field removed; malformed --writer validated. build/check/test green; cli runtime demo (unstamped item onto attested-required schema now succeeds + stamps); adversarial probe confirmed all 10 ops stamp at correct arg position, dryRun preserved, no regression; two non-functional findings fixed + re-verified. cli arc alpha; beta (FGAP-009) + gamma (FGAP-010) remain.

tasks.description: Complete the op-registry coverage of the library write surface per DEC-0006 / FGAP-009 / FGAP-006 as dual-surface units (library + op + CLI + orchestrator script): add the missing removeRelation library primitive + a remove-relation / replace-relation op (closing FGAP-006), and ops for writeRelations / upsertItemInBlock / writeConfig / writeSchema+updateSchema / registerSubstrate+writeRegistry / writeBootstrapPointer+flipBootstrapPointer / bulk appendRelations as the surface warrants; run context-validate after edge mutations. tasks.notes: Implemented at 56b153d. removeRelation primitive + removeRelationByRef/replaceRelationByRef/appendRelationsByRef porcelain + ops remove-relation/replace-relation/append-relations/upsert-block-item (all attested via alpha); OP_COVERAGE_RULE + INTENTIONALLY_UNEXPOSED_WRITERS coverage contract; foldin OP3 emits structured endpoints; OP4 unresolved-detection class-closed on substrateHasItem + drop-persist gate fix. build/check/test green; cli runtime demo (remove/replace/bulk/upsert + full step-by-step custom-substrate creation); probe->fix->re-audit loop caught a proxy false-positive + sibling-path instance + drop-persist gate bug, final re-audit clean. Per DEC-0008 / FGAP-012.

tasks.description: FGAP-013 fix — CLI --json structured output (no double-encode). Introduce OpResult = string | {json:unknown} and widen OpDefinition.run. Split serializeForRead (read-element.ts) into structureForRead (data + cap/pagination metadata, un-stringified) + renderReadText (content + footer). 19 JSON.stringify ops return {json:result}; 16 serializeForRead ops return {json: structureForRead(...)}; 14 prose ops unchanged. Shared renderOpResultText used by registerAll (Pi-tool text byte-unchanged) and the CLI default branch; CLI --json emits typeof r===string ? r : r.json. Add a cli.test contract test locking --json output as a JSON value. Per the approved plan; one subsystem (op-output contract). Full completion sequence; coding subagent; orchestrator runs all npm + sole git writer.
acceptance_criteria: --json output is a JSON value (object/array) for a read op and a JSON.stringify op; still a string for a prose op
CLI default (non-json) output and in-pi Pi-tool text output byte-unchanged
a read op past the cap emits truncated/hasMore/total as structured --json fields, no [read-element] footer inside the JSON value
new cli.test contract test locks the --json value shape
full suite green; fresh adversarial audit confirms no op left double-encoding, no prose misclassified, text surfaces unchanged

tasks.description: FGAP-015 fix — enforce the read cap at the output boundary (per DEC-0009). Three coordinated parts: (1) runtime boundary cap boundOpResult applied at the 3 emit sites (cli.ts --json :492, cli.ts text :497, registerAll Pi-tool :1927) — {read} passes through, {json}/prose serialized + byte-checked via truncateHead, over-cap fail-closes (structured {data:null,truncated:true,totalBytes,note} for --json; REFUSAL prose for text/Pi-tool); (2) route resolve-item-by-id + promote-item through structureForRead/{read} for graceful degradation; (3) parity-check gate rejecting an op that directly returns {json} of a content-reading lib call (readBlock/readBlockItem/resolveItemById/filterBlockItems/joinBlocks/resolveItemsByIds/buildIdIndex/resolveRef). Add Pi-tool over-cap test (uncovered surface). Lands with held TASK-012 as the complete --json contract; closes FGAP-013 + FGAP-015. Full completion sequence; coding subagent; orchestrator runs all npm + sole git writer + re-audit.
acceptance_criteria: over-cap {json}/prose output fail-closes on all 3 emit sites (CLI --json data:null+truncated:true no payload; CLI text + Pi-tool REFUSAL prose no payload)
resolve-item-by-id + promote-item route through {read}; over-cap degrades gracefully; under-cap full value

tasks.description: FGAP-014 fix — migrate completeTask to the verification_verifies_item edge (per FGAP-014). completeTask (context-sdk.ts) currently reads verification.target/target_type, removed by c08f93b for the edge and never migrated, so it is unusable on any catalog substrate. Rewrite as a pure edge-gate: find verification (exists), assert status===passed, find task (exists, not completed/cancelled), ASSERT a verification_verifies_item edge (parent=verificationId, child=taskId) via findReferencesInRepo/walkAncestorsByLens (throw a clear edge-absent error if missing), set task status:completed via updateItemInBlock (DROP the verification field embed — the edge is the linkage), thread ctx for attestation. complete-task op: add ctx to run() + thread. Rewrite the 12-test suite to the edge-based flow (schema-valid verifications, no smuggled target/target_type; file the edge via appendRelationByRef; wrong-task -> no-edge; drop wrong-type; add a no-edge-bypass case). Full completion sequence; coding subagent; orchestrator runs all npm + sole git writer + re-audit. Closes FGAP-014.
acceptance_criteria: completeTask gates on a verification_verifies_item edge (parent=verification, child=task) + status===passed; no read of target/target_type
a passing verification with NO edge -> throws the edge-absent error (the FGAP-014 real-substrate scenario)
task set to completed without the verification field embed; ctx-attested
test suite rewritten to schema-valid verifications + real appendRelationByRef edges (no field smuggling); drop the wrong-type test; add the no-edge-bypass case
runtime demo on a real/throwaway substrate: complete-task completes with edge+passed, throws without edge; context-validate completed-task-has-verification invariant still passes
fresh adversarial audit: gate cannot be bypassed; result cap-bounded; no regression
FGAP-014 closed with the 4 closure fields

The ops still receive + require arrayKey; the CLI supplies it.

Closes FGAP-027: pass whole:true into structureForRead at ops-registry.ts:1166 for the addressed-node case so the IN-PI read-schema op returns the full subtree (matching scripts/orchestrator/read-schema.ts:181), retaining the 50KB cap. tasks.description: Behavioral parity gate — the regression guard implementing R-0003 section F. Extends scripts/parity-check.ts with, AFTER the existing static checks: a script<->op name map (5 twins have mismatched names; 29 of 48 use-ops are currently invisible to the exact-name keying at parity-check.ts:748), fixtures + an execution harness, a required-but-derivable assertion (FAIL if an op's required param is one its script twin derives — catches the FGAP-019 class), and an output-shape diff (op --json vs script --format json on the same addressed read — the read-schema --path fixture catches the FGAP-020 class). Converts the parity class from found-one-at-a-time-by-dogfooding into caught-at-commit. Closes no gap directly; it is the durability mechanism that locks in TASK orders 1-4.
acceptance_criteria: The gate FAILs on a reintroduced required-but-derivable param (FGAP-019 class)
The gate FAILs on an op/script output-shape divergence (read-schema --path fixture, FGAP-020 class)

Green on the post-orders-1..4 tree; canonical pipeline green

tasks.description: Slice S1 (FGAP-029 P0 safety): /context install --update must NEVER overwrite a populated block. In installContext's blocks loop (packages/pi-context/src/index.ts:396-416), when destExists && overwrite, read the existing block via readBlockForDir and test for items via forEachBlockArray; if any array is non-empty, push to a new InstallResult.preserved[] and skip the copyFileSync. Empty/absent blocks behave as today; the schemas loop is UNCHANGED (schemas still honor --update). Render preserved in the install handler (index.ts:910-942). Add a regression test (none exists today). Per DEC-0010: also add a CHANGELOG [Unreleased] entry + usage-only docs (pkg README, monorepo README, SKILL via skill-narrative.md). Reuse readBlockForDir + forEachBlockArray (block-api.ts); installContext is the sole block-writer.
acceptance_criteria: --update against a populated block: block file byte-identical + reported preserved (regression test + runtime demo on a .context backup/dup per the safety protocol, never live .context)
empty existing block still overwritten; a schema under --update still updated (no regression)
no other path overwrites a populated block (fresh adversarial audit; populated-detection correct for object-shape AND flat-array blocks)
CHANGELOG [Unreleased] entry added; usage-only docs updated (README pkg + monorepo + SKILL) with NO fix-reference
canonical pipeline green: build/check/test + runtime demo + fresh adversarial audit

tasks.description: Slice S4 (FGAP-029): schema re-sync via the existing migration registry (forward-migrate, NOT blind copy) + cleaning propagation. Running it on this repo propagates the genericized catalog descriptions into .context/schemas/ (FGAP-028 residual) with ZERO data loss and resolves the layer-plans 1.0.0->1.0.1 skew. Closes FGAP-029 (data-loss closed at S1; safe schema re-sync closed here). Per DEC-0010: CHANGELOG + usage-only docs.

CHANGELOG [Unreleased] + usage-only docs
canonical pipeline green
tasks.notes: Slice S4/4 of DEC-0010 | depends on S2 | closes FGAP-029 + addresses FGAP-028 residual

tasks.description: Resolve FGAP-007: add endpoint-role metadata to ordering relation_types, enforce/normalize ordering-edge direction at write time in append-relation, reconcile relation_type name with the deriver contract, extend to the sibling ordering relations, and migrate any inverted edges.
acceptance_criteria: task_depends_on_task and the currentState deriver agree on parent/child direction: no surface where the name's implied direction is opposite the direction the deriver consumes (name reads prerequisite->dependent matching {parent=prerequisite, child=dependent}, or explicit endpoint-role metadata makes the depender/depended-upon endpoint unambiguous regardless of the verb).

The relation_type registry entry for task_depends_on_task carries explicit endpoint-role metadata identifying which endpoint is the prerequisite and which is the dependent (extending the existing source_kinds/target_kinds metadata).

append-relation validates or normalizes an ordering edge's parent/child direction against that endpoint-role metadata at write time: a direction-correct ordering edge is accepted; a backwards ordering edge is rejected or auto-oriented at append, not silently stored.

The rejection/normalization fires only for relation_types carrying the ordering endpoint-role metadata; ordering relation_types without it, and non-ordering relation_types, are unaffected (no new false-positive rejections).

Every existing task_depends_on_task edge in relations.json remains valid after the fix: none orphaned, dropped, or left inverted; any edge whose stored direction was backwards under the corrected contract is migrated to the correct orientation; any already-correct edge is unchanged.
context-validate (relations integrity) passes against the active substrate after the fix and after any edge migration, with no new integrity findings introduced.

The deriver's blocked/ready output for existing tasks is unchanged where edges were already correct, and corrected where an edge was inverted: the fix changes no correctly-derived dependency outcome and only repairs inverted ones.

The same direction enforcement applies to every sibling ordering relation_type sharing the inverted-name root (story_depends_on_story, feature_depends_on_item, feature_gated_by_item, phase_depends_on): each either carries the endpoint-role metadata and is enforced, or is demonstrably exempt because its name/derivation are already consistent; none is left with a name-vs-deriver inversion.

Tests assert the write-path direction enforcement: a backwards ordering edge is rejected/normalized, a forwards edge is accepted, the metadata-absent gate holds (no enforcement when role metadata is absent), and the deriver continues to treat {parent=prerequisite, child=dependent} correctly.
Build, check, and the full test suite pass at zero failures with the change in dist/.

tasks.description: Make pi-context a globally installable command: add a chmod +x dist/bin.js step to the pi-context-cli build so npm link and npm i -g expose an executable pi-context on PATH.
acceptance_criteria: [subagent] The pi-context-cli build sets the exec bit on the bin entry (chmod +x dist/bin.js after tsc); dist/bin.js is -rwxr-xr-x after a clean build.
[orchestrator] npm link from packages/pi-context-cli places an executable pi-context on PATH.
[orchestrator] pi-context <op> --json runs from a cwd outside the repo, resolving @davidorex/pi-context through the linked dependency tree.
[orchestrator] build + check + full test suite pass at zero failures.
[orchestrator] published-package global install (npm i -g @davidorex/pi-context-cli) smoke-tested from a clean machine/dir — deferred, release-gated.

tasks.description: Declare @davidorex/pi-project-workflows in pi-context-cli dependencies at the lockstep version range so pi-context --pi-bound's require.resolve('@davidorex/pi-project-workflows/package.json') resolves from an installed CLI, not only via the in-repo workspace symlink.
acceptance_criteria: [subagent] pi-context-cli declares @davidorex/pi-project-workflows at the lockstep version range in dependencies.
[orchestrator] require.resolve('@davidorex/pi-project-workflows/package.json') resolves under npm link from a cwd outside the repo.
[orchestrator] build + check + full test suite pass; lockstep version ranges across CLI -> pi-context -> meta remain internally consistent.
[orchestrator] registry-install resolution of the meta-package verified — deferred, release-gated.

tasks.description: Implement pi-context pi-bound per analysis/2026-06-07-pi-context-pi-bound-cli.md: a CLI process mode that launches an interactive pi session bound to the composed extension tool surface, reproducing scripts/launch-constrained-pi.sh.
acceptance_criteria: [subagent] pi-bound is routed in cli.ts main() before op resolution (dispatched when the first positional is 'pi-bound') and excluded from the reflected surface:use op set.
[subagent] The launched session's tool surface is the union of the two-package-root SKILL.md tool set (the script's 69 tools) plus the per-target composites from config.tool_operations[].
[subagent] The meta-package root is resolved via require.resolve('@davidorex/pi-project-workflows/package.json'); pi install -l <metaRoot> runs against it; the SKILL.md union globs that root.
[subagent] Context is composed in-process via loadContext (no tsx or python3 subprocess), per DEC-0015.
[subagent] --grant <id> extends the tool union; a trailing --grant with no value exits 2; passthrough arguments reach pi unchanged.
[subagent] pi is launched restricted to --tools <union>; the union is re-derived every launch (no stale reuse on resume).

[orchestrator] Runtime demo: pi-context pi-bound launches an interactive pi session whose available tools equal the composed union and exclude tools outside it — equivalent to scripts/launch-constrained-pi.sh.
[orchestrator] build + check + full test suite pass at zero failures; the change is built into dist/.
[orchestrator] published-package smoke test of the meta-path resolution + pi install -l from a registry install — deferred, release-gated.

tasks.description: Register the two governance relation_types in .context config and backfill all existing planning-artifact articulation: file the deduped missing-convention gaps and write the item_governed_by_convention / item_acknowledges_missing_convention edges for every existing decision/feature/task and for this initiative's own DEC/FEAT/TASK items, per analysis/2026-06-07-convention-articulation-backfill.md. Prerequisite to enabling the error invariant (TASK-032).
acceptance_criteria: [orchestrator] item_governed_by_convention (source decisions/features/tasks -> target conventions) and item_acknowledges_missing_convention (source decisions/features/tasks -> target framework-gaps) are registered in .context config.relation_types.
[orchestrator] The deduped missing-convention gaps (one per theme) are filed.

[orchestrator] Every existing decision/feature/task, plus this initiative's own DEC-0016/FEAT-007/TASK-031/032/033, carries at least one articulation edge (governed-by-convention or acknowledges-missing-convention) per the backfill determination.
[orchestrator] context-validate shows no unregistered-relation_type or dangling-edge errors after the writes.

tasks.description: Install the three requires-edge invariants (one per source block: decisions, features, tasks) in .context config at severity error, each requiring at least one item_governed_by_convention or item_acknowledges_missing_convention edge as_parent. Enact DEC-0016. Run only after the backfill (TASK-031) so the substrate validates clean.
acceptance_criteria: [orchestrator] Three requires-edge invariants are added to .context config.invariants (block decisions / features / tasks; relation_types both governance types; direction as_parent; severity error).
[orchestrator] context-validate reports zero convention-articulation errors (the backfill made it clean).
[orchestrator] A new decision/feature/task filed without an articulation edge is rejected by context-validate as an error.
[orchestrator] DEC-0016 is enacted (status + enacted_by + enacted_at + closing-commit reference).

tasks.description: Ship the governance vocabulary in the samples catalog: add the two relation_types and three requires-edge invariants (severity warning, for fresh substrates) to packages/pi-context/samples/conception.json, with CHANGELOG + usage docs, then release. Fresh install/accept-all substrates then carry advisory convention-articulation.
acceptance_criteria: [subagent] samples/conception.json declares the two governance relation_types and three requires-edge invariants at severity warning.
[orchestrator] A fresh install/accept-all substrate carries the vocabulary; context-validate WARNS (does not error) on an unarticulated planning artifact.
[orchestrator] CHANGELOG [Unreleased] + usage docs updated; build + check + full test suite green.
[orchestrator] released via npm run release:* (authorization-gated).

tasks.description: FEAT-006 T1 — the pi-context update command shell + drift routing. Add a bare-verb `pi-context update` (per cli-command-form) with a `--dry-run` verb flag, superseding `install --update` as the user-facing update path. Update consults checkStatus (index.ts:858) per installed schema and routes by drift state: in-sync / catalog-ahead -> the existing resync path; locally-modified / both-diverged -> refuse-and-report (do NOT overwrite) until T3 (TASK-036) upgrades that branch to merge. This slice delivers 'never silently clobber' immediately via refuse-and-report.
acceptance_criteria: [subagent] pi-context update is a bare verb routed in cli.ts before op resolution, excluded from the surface:use reflection, with --dry-run a verb flag (per cli-command-form).

[subagent] update consults checkStatus per installed schema and branches on state: in-sync/catalog-ahead -> existing resync path; locally-modified/both-diverged -> refuse-and-report (schema left unmodified).
[orchestrator] a locally-modified schema is NOT overwritten by update; the user's edits survive (demonstrated against an edited installed schema).

[orchestrator] pi-context update --dry-run prints the per-schema drift state + intended action and writes nothing; in-pi ops byte-unchanged; build + check + full test pass; runtime demo on a fresh + an existing substrate.

tasks.description: FEAT-006 T2 — base-stamping (the merge precondition). On every install/update, store the as-installed catalog schema BODY via the content-addressed object store: putObject(destRoot, asset.content_hash, schemaJson) at the baseline write (index.ts:738-754), keyed by the content_hash the asset already records (object-store.ts; content-hash.ts:82-84). Retrievable at merge time via getObject(destRoot, content_hash). This supplies the 3-way merge base that does not exist on disk today (checkStatus keeps only a SHA-256, context.ts:79). Depends on TASK-034.
acceptance_criteria: [subagent] every install/update writes the as-installed schema body to the object store via putObject keyed by the asset's recorded content_hash; installed_from.assets shape is unchanged (no context.ts:79 type change).
[orchestrator] after install, getObject(destRoot, assets[name].content_hash) returns a body that JCS-canonicalizes back to that content_hash (round-trip).
[orchestrator] purely additive: checkStatus classifications are byte-identical to before base-stamping; build + check + full test pass; runtime demo (install -> base body present + retrievable).

tasks.description: FEAT-006 T3 — deterministic schema-merge. Add a key/path-level draft-07 3-way merge mergeSchema(base, ours, theirs) -> {merged, conflicts[]}: base = the base-stamped as-installed body (getObject), ours = the installed dest schema, theirs = the catalog source schema. Wire it into the update flow at the locally-modified / both-diverged branch (replacing TASK-034's refuse-and-report): auto-apply the merged schema when the conflict set is empty; on a non-empty conflict set leave the schema unmodified and hand the typed set to the resolver (TASK-037). Depends on TASK-035; governed by DEC-0017.
acceptance_criteria: [subagent] mergeSchema(base, ours, theirs) returns {merged, conflicts[]}: disjoint key additions merge; required / enum / type-union arrays merge as sets; overlapping edits to the same node emit a typed conflict {path, base, ours, theirs}.
[subagent] the merged output passes AJV draft-07 meta-validation + assertNoNestedIdBearingArray before any write.
[orchestrator] a locally-modified schema with only disjoint edits: update auto-applies the merge and BOTH the user's edit and the catalog change survive (demo: add-field, widen-enum).
[orchestrator] an overlapping edit: the schema is left unmodified and the typed conflict set is produced (no clobber); checkStatus is consulted before merging (closes FGAP-049).
[orchestrator] deterministic — identical (base, ours, theirs) yield identical {merged, conflicts}; build + check + full test pass; runtime demo of a clean-merge and a conflict case.

tasks.description: FEAT-006 T4 — conflict resolver. Route the typed conflict set from TASK-036 to a resolver: a pi-bound agent mergetool — dispatch a bounded pi-bound session granted read-schema + write-schema, feeding (base, ours, theirs); the agent proposes a reconciled schema and writes it via write-schema, forced through the existing dryRun + auth-gate (non-interactive refuses) — with graceful fallback to a rendered conflict report {path, base, ours, theirs} when no interactive/credentialed pi is available. The schema is left unmodified on the report path; the rest of the update proceeds either way. No change to auth-gate or pi-bound internals (grant + dispatch only). Depends on TASK-036; governed by DEC-0017.
acceptance_criteria: [subagent] on a non-empty conflict set, update dispatches a bounded pi-bound session granted read-schema + write-schema, feeding (base, ours, theirs); the agent's write passes through the existing write-schema dryRun + auth-gate (non-interactive refuses) with no change to auth-gate or pi-bound internals.

[subagent] with no interactive context, update falls back to a rendered conflict report {path, base, ours, theirs}, leaves the schema unmodified, and proceeds with the rest of the update (never hard-blocks).

[orchestrator] runtime demo: interactive -> an agent proposal previewed via --dry-run and applied on confirm; non-interactive -> a clear conflict report + schema unchanged + remaining schemas still updated.
[orchestrator] build + check + full test pass.

tasks.description: FEAT-006 T5 — config-registry propagation. On update, additively merge catalog-new config-registry entries (relation_types, invariants, block_kinds, lenses) that are ABSENT from the existing substrate config into it, preserving the user's existing/modified registries; surfaced in output and previewed by --dry-run. Independent of the schema-merge slices (disjoint seam: the installContext config spread, the FGAP-060 site). Closes FGAP-060.
acceptance_criteria: [subagent] update additively merges catalog-new config-registry entries (relation_types / invariants / block_kinds / lenses) absent from the existing config; it never removes or overwrites a user-present entry.

[orchestrator] an existing substrate gains the new catalog vocabulary on update; user-added/-modified registry entries are untouched; --dry-run lists the additions and writes nothing (closes FGAP-060).
[orchestrator] build + check + full test pass; runtime demo (existing substrate + a catalog carrying new entries -> entries appear, customizations intact).

tasks.description: FEAT-006 T6 — surfaced mutation reporting + idempotent skip. The update output enumerates every mutation it performs (schema re-syncs/merges, migration-declaration registrations into migrations.json); --dry-run lists them without writing. And update skips rewriting an existing catalog-origin block file whose content already equals the starter. Independent of the schema-merge slices (disjoint seam: the install summary + the block branch). Closes FGAP-050 + FGAP-051.
acceptance_criteria: [subagent] update output enumerates every schema re-sync/merge and every migration declaration registered into migrations.json; --dry-run lists them without writing (closes FGAP-050).
[subagent] update skips rewriting an existing catalog-origin block file whose content already equals the starter (closes FGAP-051).
[orchestrator] runtime demo: a version-bump update lists the registered migration declarations; an unchanged empty block is not rewritten; build + check + full test pass.
